title: Wildfire Smokes Makes Air Hazardous for Millions in Midwest and East Coast
summary: The haze is likely to ease in the East this weekend but linger in the Upper Midwest, where some cities recorded extremely high pollution indexes on Thursday.

title: Canada’s Wildfires Are Burning Through the Night
summary: As overnight temperatures climb across Canada, fires that once spread more slowly after dark are burning around the clock, straining fire crews.

title: What to Know About Canada Wildfire Smoke and Air Quality in U.S. Cities
summary: A thick plume of smoke swept across the Midwest and New England, and those conditions could linger on Friday.

title: Ukraine Was on a Roll. Then a Clash Over War Strategy Exploded Into View.

title: He Was a Russian Political Survivor, Until the Masked Men Appeared
summary: Boris B. Nadezhdin, an outspoken opponent of the war in Ukraine, says he knows why he was detained: “Among our leadership, there is panic and chaos.”

title: Takeaways From Trump’s Speech Claiming Election Vulnerabilities and China Interference
summary: Documents released by the Trump administration to support the president’s claims did not back up his most aggressive statements.

title: A Trump Obsession That Carries a Cost for Democracy
summary: In demanding steps to address the integrity of voting, President Trump persisted in relitigating his 2020 election defeat while finding ways to cast doubt on the 2026 outcome.

title: Trump Has Dismantled Election Security Efforts. Here’s How.
summary: Since his return to the White House, the lead federal partner for states on election security has lost around a third of its work force.

title: Trump Released Documents on China and the 2020 Election. Here’s What We Know.
summary: President Trump claimed that China had tried to acquire American voter data. Possessing such information would not allow votes to be manipulated.

title: Cyclospora Linked to Taylor Farms Lettuce Sent to Taco Bell
summary: Federal officials worked with Michigan investigators to trace the outbreak to iceberg lettuce, which may also have gone to other vendors.

summary: It’s the first potentially habitable world known to host an atmosphere, making it a lead contender in the search for life beyond our solar system.

summary: Conversations with economists, and people around him, shed light on how Andy Burnham might tackle entrenched challenges.

summary: Andy Burnham is set to officially become the next prime minister on Monday, but a number of things have to happen before then. Here’s how everything is expected to play out.

title: Republican Rifts Deepen Over $95 Billion Budget Plan for Iran War and SAVE Act
summary: A House panel approved the measure. But Republicans are divided over its size, cost and policy provisions, and many are concerned about the timing before the midterm elections.

title: Manhattan Building’s Buckled Columns Probably Lacked Required Reinforcements
summary: Drawings obtained by The New York Times show the columns called for additional steel plates, but engineers reviewing site photos said they appeared to be missing.

summary: Senator Thom Tillis of North Carolina, a key vote on confirmation, had demanded the acting attorney general meet with victims.

summary: A technical assistant to President Trump won around $100,000, according to the prediction market, which flagged the activity to the federal government.

title: Trump Administration Restricts Green Cards for Immigrants on Public Assistance
summary: The move is a significant change that could deter hundreds of thousands of immigrants from using such programs to meet basic needs.

title: Johan Guerrero, Maine ICE Shooting Victim, Relentlessly Pursued a Better Life, Partner Says
summary: The partner of Johan Guerrero, who was killed by an immigration agent this week, described him as a tireless worker and doting father who would do anything for his 3-year-old daughter.

title: Houston ICE Shooting: Lawyer Says Bags Held Salt, Not Drugs
summary: Federal investigators said a “crystal-like” substance in the bags looked like methamphetamine. A lawyer for the victim’s brother said it was salt.

title: Chinese Human Rights Lawyer Seeking Asylum Is Arrested by ICE
summary: Shaoping Wu was working as a contract delivery driver for Amazon when he was pulled over in Pennsylvania. He had championed personal and religious freedom for Chinese citizens.

title: For Families Facing a Deadly Brain Cancer, Scientific Progress Can’t Come Soon Enough
summary: Researchers have made important strides in understanding and treating diffuse midline gliomas, which were long seen as a death sentence for the children and adolescents they affect.

summary: For the country, Andy Burnham looks a lot like more of the same.

title: Two Dead and Scores Rescued as Flooding Engulfs Central Texas
summary: Flooding from relentless rain, which Gov. Greg Abbott said was likely to break records, left a trail of damage in many communities in the Hill Country region.
